evidence_text: Kimi K2.5 was continued-pretrained on approximately 15 trillion mixed visual and text tokens and is described as a native multimodal model.
source_excerpt: "Kimi K2.5 builds on Kimi K2 with continued pretraining over approximately 15T mixed visual and text tokens. Built as a native multimodal model"
note:
This is a source-reported training description from the vendor blog.
```
caveat:
This is a source-reported training description from the vendor blog.
```

evidence_text: For complex tasks, Kimi K2.5 is described as self-directing an agent swarm with up to 100 sub-agents and up to 1,500 tool calls.
source_excerpt: "For complex tasks, Kimi K2.5 can self-direct an agent swarm with up to 100 sub-agents, executing parallel workflows across up to 1,500 tool calls."
note:
This is a capability claim stated directly in the source.
```
caveat:
This is a capability claim stated directly in the source.
```

evidence_text: The source claims the agent swarm reduces execution time by up to 4.5× versus a single-agent setup.
source_excerpt: "Compared with a single-agent setup, this reduces execution time by up to 4.5x."
note:
The comparison basis is stated in the blog but not independently validated in the captured source.
```
caveat:
The comparison basis is stated in the blog but not independently validated in the captured source.
```

evidence_text: K2.5 Agent Swarm is described as beta on Kimi.com, with free credits available to high-tier paid users.
source_excerpt: "Agent Swarm is currently in beta on Kimi.com, with free credits available for high-tier paid users."
note:
This is a product-availability and access-status statement.
```
caveat:
This is a product-availability and access-status statement.
```

evidence_text: The source says Kimi Code is open-sourced, runs in the terminal, integrates with IDEs including VSCode, Cursor, and Zed, and accepts image and video inputs.
source_excerpt: "Kimi Code works in your terminal and can be integrated with various IDEs including VSCode, Cursor, Zed, etc. Kimi Code is open-sourced and supports images and videos as inputs."
note:
This evidence is limited to the product description in the source.
```
caveat:
This evidence is limited to the product description in the source.
```

evidence_text: The source describes Parallel-Agent Reinforcement Learning as using a trainable orchestrator to decompose tasks into parallel subtasks executed by dynamically instantiated frozen subagents.
source_excerpt: "PARL uses a trainable orchestrator agent to decompose tasks into parallelizable subtasks, each executed by dynamically instantiated, frozen subagents."
note:
This is a direct mechanism description from the source.
```
caveat:
This is a direct mechanism description from the source.
```

evidence_text: The source identifies serial collapse as a failure mode in parallel orchestration and says PARL uses staged reward shaping to encourage parallelism early and shift toward task success later.
source_excerpt: "A common failure mode is serial collapse, where the orchestrator defaults to single-agent execution despite having parallel capacity. To address this, PARL employs staged reward shaping that encourages parallelism early in training and gradually shifts focus toward task success."
note:
This evidence matters because the source itself states a failure mode and the training intervention meant to counter it.
```
caveat:
This evidence matters because the source itself states a failure mode and the training intervention meant to counter it.
```

evidence_text: The source says evaluation uses a latency-oriented Critical Steps metric that adds orchestration overhead to the slowest subagent at each stage.
source_excerpt: "Instead of counting total steps, we evaluate performance using Critical Steps, a latency-oriented metric inspired by the critical path in parallel computation: CriticalSteps = ∑ t = 1 T ( S main ( t ) + max i S sub , i ( t ) ) S main ( t ) captures orchestration overhead, while max i S sub , i ( t ) reflects the slowest subagent at each stage."
note:
This evidence directly states the evaluation metric used for parallel execution.
```
caveat:
This evidence directly states the evaluation metric used for parallel execution.
```

evidence_text: In internal evaluations, the source says K2.5 Agent Swarm produced an 80% reduction in end-to-end runtime and reduced minimum critical steps by 3× to 4.5× versus single-agent execution in wide-search scenarios.
source_excerpt: "In our internal evaluations, it leads to an 80% reduction in end-to-end runtime while enabling more complex, long-horizon workloads" and "Agent Swarm reduces the minimum critical steps required to achieve target performance by 3×–4.5× compared to single-agent execution in wide search scenario"
note:
The source explicitly labels these as internal evaluations, so the measurement context should remain attached downstream.
```
caveat:
The source explicitly labels these as internal evaluations, so the measurement context should remain attached downstream.
```

evidence_text: The source says K2.5 improved over K2 Thinking by 59.3% on the AI Office benchmark and 24.3% on the General Agent benchmark.
source_excerpt: "Across both benchmarks, K2.5 shows 59.3% and 24.3% improvements over K2 Thinking"
note:
These are source-reported results on internal expert productivity benchmarks rather than externally described public benchmarks in the captured text.
```
caveat:
These are source-reported results on internal expert productivity benchmarks rather than externally described public benchmarks in the captured text.
```

evidence_text: The source says K2.5 Agent supports adding annotations in Word, building financial models with Pivot Tables, writing LaTeX equations in PDFs, and scaling to outputs such as 10,000-word papers or 100-page documents.
source_excerpt: "K2.5 agent supports advanced tasks such as adding annotations in Word, constructing financial models with Pivot Tables, and writing LaTeX equations in PDFs, while scaling to long-form outputs like 10,000-word papers or 100-page documents."
note:
This is a capability description stated directly in the source.
```
caveat:
This is a capability description stated directly in the source.
```

evidence_text: Unless otherwise specified, the source says Kimi K2.5 experiments used temperature 1.0, top-p 0.95, and a 256k-token context length.
source_excerpt: "Unless otherwise specified, all Kimi K2.5 experiments were conducted with temperature = 1.0, top-p = 0.95, and a context length of 256k tokens."
note: This benchmark-setting detail bounds how the reported results should be interpreted and compared.
caveat: This benchmark-setting detail bounds how the reported results should be interpreted and compared.
